figures.caption: Table 6: Training hyperparameters for NaturalReasoning-SFT/ARES-SFT and CPT baselines.

Figure 1: Overview of the six-stage ARES pipeline. Starting from raw pretraining documents, ARES performs document filtering, domain and persona conditioning, rubric-augmented QA generation, quality verification, rubric validation, and format conversion to produce training instances.
Table 11: Licenses for existing assets used in this work.
Figure 4: Per-benchmark comparison between CPT and ARES-RL.
Figure 5: A representative ARES-generated instance from the Medicine & Health domain.

Table 1: Comparison of existing datasets and RL training pipelines across four key desiderata. ✓ indicates the property is satisfied; × indicates it is not; / indicates the property is not applicable. Multi-Domain: covers diverse domains beyond math and code. Rubric Rewards: provides multidimensional rubric-based reward signals rather than binary verification. Doc-Grounded: questions and answers are grounded in source pretraining documents. Scalable: the pipeline can be readily scaled to larger data without manual curation. ARES is the only approach satisfying all four criteria.
Table 10: Reward-design ablation results with 95% confidence intervals (open-ended tasks).
